findings.title: Diff parity coverage omits required exclusion cases
findings.detail: The spec/proposal requires `filteredDiff()` parity with the current filter behavior, including excluded-file accounting beyond the happy path: deleted files must be omitted from the patch but recorded in `summary.excluded`, untracked files remain out of scope, and pure renames need the expected excluded metadata. The design/tasks only call out generic parity plus pure renames, so an implementer could miss deleted/untracked handling or change the serialized `DiffSummary` shape without a failing task. Add explicit design notes and tests for deleted files, untracked files, excluded counts/warnings, and the exact summary output contract used by `specflow-filter-diff`.

findings.title: Originless repo fallback no longer matches the approved contract
findings.detail: The proposal explicitly defines the no-`origin` fallback as `local/<directory-name>` for `projectIdentity()` and says `projectDisplayName()` should use the same fallback, but D8 changes the identifier to a hashed form. That is a behavioral change to persisted metadata for originless repositories and can break parity/backward-compatibility assumptions. Either keep the specified fallback or amend the spec first and add explicit tests for the chosen fallback behavior.

findings.title: Detached-HEAD behavior conflicts with the stated WorkspaceContext contract
findings.detail: The proposal/spec define LocalWorkspaceContext.branchName() in terms of `git branch --show-current`, which yields `null`/empty when the branch cannot be determined, but D9 and tasks 2.4/7.1a intentionally switch to `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` and return `"HEAD"`. That leaves the interface contract, local implementation, and tests disagreeing on observable behavior. Decide which behavior is authoritative, then align the design and tasks to that single contract before implementation.

findings.title: Shared DiffSummary ownership is left undefined
findings.detail: D3 says WorkspaceContext will reuse the existing `DiffSummary` type from `specflow-filter-diff`, while D5 turns `specflow-filter-diff` into a thin CLI wrapper. The design never states where `DiffSummary` will live after that refactor. If the type remains owned by the CLI module, `src/lib/workspace-context.ts` will depend on a binary/wrapper module or create an import cycle, which breaks the intended layering. Define a shared library location for `DiffSummary` and add an explicit task to move/update imports.

findings.title: CLI failure-path compatibility is underspecified
findings.detail: `LocalWorkspaceContext` is required to fail during construction for invalid workspaces, but the design does not define how `specflow-run start`, `specflow-review-apply`, and `specflow-filter-diff` will catch and surface that exception while preserving existing CLI stderr/exit-code behavior. A constructor unit test alone does not satisfy Acceptance Criteria 5 and 6. Specify the CLI error-handling path and add integration tests for invalid-workspace execution to verify clear messages and non-breaking exit semantics.

findings.title: Factory requires the value it is supposed to resolve
findings.detail: `createLocalWorkspaceContext(projectRoot: string)` is circular with the interface contract: `WorkspaceContext.projectRoot()` is supposed to resolve the git root, but the factory as written implies the CLI must already know that root before construction. That leaves the ownership of workspace validation/root resolution ambiguous and weakens the intended abstraction boundary. Change the constructor/factory to accept `cwd`/`workspacePath` (or no argument) and have `LocalWorkspaceContext` resolve and validate the root internally; if you keep the factory pattern, make it a thin wrapper around an actual `LocalWorkspaceContext` class so the design still matches the spec.
